steps.title: Substitute y = 19 into both equations
steps.content:
Since the intersection point is $(q, 19)$, we know $y = 19$ at this point. Substitute $y = 19$ into both original equations:

Equation 1: $-x - w(19) = -337$ becomes $-x - 19w = -337$
Equation 2: $2x - w(19) = 47$ becomes $2x - 19w = 47$

Now we have a system of two equations in two unknowns ($x$ and $w$).

steps.title: Subtract equations to eliminate w
steps.content:
Subtract equation 1 from equation 2 to eliminate the $w$ term:

$$(2x - 19w) - (-x - 19w) = 47 - (-337)$$

$$3x = 384$$

$$x = 128$$

So the x-coordinate of the intersection point is $q = 128$.
